inputs.label: Value 1
inputs.businessContext: First numeric value to be averaged. Used in arithmetic mean calculation.

inputs.label: Value 2
inputs.businessContext: Second numeric value to be averaged.

inputs.label: Value 3
inputs.businessContext: Third numeric value to be averaged.

inputs.label: Value 4
inputs.businessContext: Fourth numeric value to be averaged.

inputs.label: Value 5
inputs.businessContext: Fifth numeric value to be averaged.

rules: All input values must be finite numbers.
At least one input must be non-zero to compute average (count > 0).

thresholds.countZero: If count is 0, average is undefined.

formulas.count: count = (value1 !== undefined ? 1 : 0) + (value2 !== undefined ? 1 : 0) + (value3 !== undefined ? 1 : 0) + (value4 !== undefined ? 1 : 0) + (value5 !== undefined ? 1 : 0)

suggestedActions: Ensure all input values are accurate and represent the same unit of measure.
If average is used for benchmarking, compare against industry standards.

premiumFeatures: Detailed report with statistical measures (median, mode, standard deviation)
